summary: Julia Evans documents a brief foray into WebAssembly during her Recurse Center batch. She paired with others to explore creating art in Rust compiled to WebAssembly and found the setup process surprisingly simple. The post notes the initially confusing use of npm to build a Rust program, and mentions compilation times on different machines.
key_points: Getting started with WebAssembly was easier than expected, requiring just a git clone and npm install
The npm-based build process for a Rust WebAssembly project can be initially confusing since it hides cargo commands behind the scenes
Compilation times varied significantly between machines, from 2 to 10 minutes
money_quotes: I learned that it's easier to get started with WebAssembly than I thought
At first I was confused because the instructions said to use `npm`, but this is a Rust program! What's going on!
key_insight: Getting started with Rust and WebAssembly is more approachable than expected, requiring only standard npm tooling to compile and serve a working example.
